title: Red Hat Security Advisory: mariadb-devel:10.3 security update
overview:
An update for the mariadb-devel:10.3 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise
Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of
Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives
a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE
link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: mariadb-devel:10.3 security update

An update for the mariadb-devel:10.3 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise
Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of
Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives
a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE
link(s) in the References section.

An update for the mariadb-devel:10.3 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise
Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of
Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives
a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE
link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
ad5852d3.src.rpm
ad5852d3.i686.rpm
ad5852d3.x86_64.rpm
5eac0e54.i686.rpm
ad5852d3.ppc64le.rpm
ad5852d3.aarch64.rpm
ad5852d3.s390x.rpm

CVE-2025-13699: A flaw was found in MariaDB. This vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected installations via improper validation of a user-supplied path prior to using it in file operations in the mariadb-dump utility, requiring user interaction.
